themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their focus on 'national priority' policies, which prioritize citizens over immigrants in access to public services, housing, and employment. This theme is prominently featured in Spain (VOX), Italy (Fratelli d'Italia/Lega), and France (Rassemblement National), with parallel debates in regional governments and municipal councils.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: VOX campaigns on 'national priority' in Andalusia, demanding preferential treatment for Spaniards in public aid, housing, and employment. The party has successfully pressured PP-led regional governments to adopt these policies in Aragón and Castilla y León.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega and Fratelli d'Italia have clashed over housing and immigration policies, with Lega pushing for stricter controls on public housing allocations to prioritize Italian citizens. The Meloni government has also faced criticism for adopting similar rhetoric in national policies.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Jordan Bardella and Marine Le Pen have emphasized 'national preference' in public services, criticizing immigration policies and advocating for stricter citizenship requirements. Bardella's stance on halal menus in schools and healthcare access for immigrants reflects this priority.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Nigel Farage and Reform UK have adopted similar rhetoric, advocating for stricter immigration controls and prioritizing British citizens in public services. The party's local election campaigns emphasize these policies as central to their platform.

themes.title: Trump’s policies dominate global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Donald Trump's domestic and foreign policies continue to shape right-wing discourse worldwide, with his administration's decisions on immigration, taxation, and military engagements serving as a reference point for right-wing and far-right parties. Coverage spans the U.S., UK, Spain, Italy, and France, reflecting Trump's enduring influence on global conservative movements.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's policies on immigration, taxation, and military engagements dominate U.S. right-wing media. His administration's decisions, such as renaming federal agencies, capping student loans, and escalating tensions with Iran, are frequently cited as models for conservative governance.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK frequently references Trump's immigration policies, advocating for stricter border controls and reduced migration. Nigel Farage has praised Trump's approach to negotiating with foreign leaders, positioning it as a model for UK policy.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: VOX has aligned its immigration and economic policies with Trump's rhetoric, particularly on prioritizing national citizens in public services and opposing globalist trade agreements. The party has also criticized Spain's immigration policies using Trump's 'America First' framing.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing parties have adopted Trump's anti-immigration and pro-nationalist rhetoric, with Meloni's government facing criticism for aligning with Trump's foreign policy stance on Iran and Israel. Salvini has also praised Trump's economic policies, particularly on taxation.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella have referenced Trump's policies on immigration and economic nationalism, advocating for similar measures in France. Bardella's criticism of the EU and support for stricter immigration controls mirror Trump's approach.

themes.title: Hungary’s political shift under new leadership
themes.blurb: Hungary's recent election has ushered in a new political era under Péter Magyar and the Tisza Party, ending Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule. The transition has sparked debates about corruption, EU relations, and domestic reforms, with right-wing and far-right parties in Poland, Germany, and the U.S. closely monitoring the developments.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Péter Magyar's Tisza Party has taken power, promising to dismantle Orbán's legacy by restoring rule of law, combating corruption, and unlocking frozen EU funds. The new government's appointments and policy shifts are closely scrutinized by domestic and international observers.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Poland's PiS has expressed concerns about Hungary's political shift, particularly regarding potential impacts on regional alliances and EU dynamics. The party has also drawn parallels between Hungary's transition and Poland's own political struggles.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD has commented on Hungary's political transition, framing it as a cautionary tale for Germany's own political landscape. The party has highlighted the challenges of combating corruption and restoring democratic norms.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump and his allies have praised Orbán's leadership, and the shift in Hungary has sparked discussions about the future of U.S.-Hungary relations. Some MAGA figures have expressed skepticism about the new government's ability to maintain Hungary's nationalist policies.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Far-right rhetoric continues to be linked to incidents of political violence and extremism, with recent cases involving eco-fascists, neo-Nazis, and far-right activists targeting mosques, politicians, and journalists. This theme has resonated in Italy, the UK, the U.S., and Spain, where far-right parties and movements are increasingly associated with violent acts.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italy has seen a rise in far-right rhetoric linked to political violence, with Lega and Fratelli d'Italia members accused of inciting hatred. Recent incidents include threats against journalists and protests targeting pro-Palestinian activists.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK has faced criticism for its members' rhetoric, which has been linked to incidents of harassment and threats against political opponents. Nigel Farage's statements on immigration and national security have also been scrutinized for potentially inciting violence.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's rhetoric has been linked to multiple incidents of political violence, including assassination attempts and threats against journalists. MAGA supporters have been involved in protests that escalated into violence, particularly during the 2024 election cycle.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: VOX has been accused of fueling political violence through its rhetoric on immigration and national identity. The party's members have faced legal action for threats against political opponents, and its policies have sparked protests and counter-protests.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Far-right parties across Europe are experiencing a surge in polling numbers, with significant gains in Germany, France, Sweden, the UK, and Norway. This trend reflects growing public dissatisfaction with mainstream parties and a shift toward nationalist, anti-immigration, and anti-EU platforms.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD continues to gain traction in German polls, particularly in regional elections. The party's focus on immigration, EU skepticism, and economic nationalism has resonated with voters disillusioned with mainstream parties.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella have led RN to record-high polling numbers, positioning the party as a frontrunner for the 2027 presidential election. RN's anti-immigration and anti-EU stance has gained significant support.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Sweden Democrats have solidified their position as a major political force, with recent polling showing them as the second-largest party. The party's focus on immigration and law-and-order policies has driven its popularity.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK has seen a surge in support, particularly in local elections, where the party is positioning itself as a viable alternative to the Conservatives. Nigel Farage's leadership and anti-immigration stance have been key drivers of this growth.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP has gained significant support in Norway, with polls showing the party as a leading contender in upcoming elections. The party's focus on immigration, taxation, and energy policies has resonated with voters.
